mechanism_narrative: A decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly boosting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate cuts are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions, which reduces consumer spending expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or out-of-sample accuracy drops below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in retail sales and stable consumer price inflation indicates robust consumer demand without pricing pressure, supporting corporate revenue growth and yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to produce a positive mean forward return over 60 days in backtests, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during an economic expansion phase reflects strengthening supply-side capacity and corporate output, which translates into higher earnings expectations and positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The signal generates negative forward returns during expansion phases, or the predictive power vanishes when filtered to non-expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: Strong month-over-month nonfarm payroll additions reflect immediate economic vitality and wage growth, creating short-term bullish sentiment that pushes S&P 500 returns higher within the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following strong payroll prints are not significantly positive, or the signal exhibits negative autocorrelation in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in housing starts below the 12-month moving average signals weakening residential investment and broader demand contraction, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below its 12-month moving average
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns remain positive in > 60% of instances when housing starts drop below the trend, invalidating the contraction signal.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price inflation combined with a stagnant federal funds rate compresses real interest rates, prompting expectations of aggressive future tightening that depresses equity valuations and yields negative forward returns.
failure_condition: The interaction between inflation and rates fails to predict negative returns, or the model produces positive alpha when real rates are compressed.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained quarterly GDP growth above 2.5% during a late-cycle expansion confirms resilient aggregate demand, reinforcing earnings forecasts and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: GDP growth above 2.5% in late-cycle periods is followed by negative 60-day returns, or the signal loses predictive power when regime-filtered.
